title: Resource Graphs, Cache Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Non-Claims · Execution Trace: Performance Nonclaim
summary: The tool reports document bytes as total page weight, ignores encoded versus decoded sizes and browser discovery, and turns one server timing into user performance. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go performance nonclaim increment in static resource and delivery evidence dossier. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims.
Set page-class count, byte, third-party, image, font, script, and uncertainty budgets with owners and exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Performance Nonclaim · Theory Performance Nonclaim
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 9157 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
